summary: Stancil argues that OLAP cubes persist as a conceptual “ghost” in modern BI tools: even when the infrastructure no longer requires cubes, interfaces still expose confusing, cube-like abstractions. He contends that BI should hide internal data structure and instead present data in the simple forms users actually think in—metrics and flat tables—so tools are understandable without implicit knowledge.
money_quotes: OLAP cubes are just tables, but tables structured in a very particular way.
But there’s another, much less discussed downside to OLAP cubes: They’re very difficult to understand.
Rather than displaying data as we model it, we should display data as we use it.
Most BI tools look like the Census site.
After three decades of trying with OLAP cubes, it’s time to give up the ghost.
key_insight: Even as OLAP cubes fade technically, BI tools still mirror their confusing abstractions, so we should redesign interfaces around how users ask questions—metrics and flat tables—rather than how data is modeled.
